steps.title: Track the repeated sameness
steps.content: The text describes Coketown through repetition: streets are like one another, people are like one another, and residents follow the same hours, sounds, work, days, and years. The main purpose must capture that broad pattern of uniformity.

steps.title: Reject narrower purposes
steps.content:
• Choice B - 'To explain the limited work opportunities available to the town's residents': focuses on work, but the text never explains job options and work is only one part of the repeated sameness.
  • Choice C - 'To reveal how the predictability of the town makes it easy for people to lose track of time': adds an effect the text never states; the repeated days and years show sameness, not confusion about time.
  • Choice D - 'To argue that the simplicity of life in the town makes it a pleasant place to live': invents a positive judgment that the passage does not make.

steps.title: State the main purpose
steps.content: Choice A - 'To emphasize the uniformity of both the town and the people who live there': matches the repeated description of similar streets, similar residents, shared routines, and days that resemble one another. The answer is A.
